tests.description: generateCubeLattice accepts seed and jitter options and produces non-uniform vertex positions
generateCubeLattice with jitter=0 produces the same positions as without jitter
Jitter is deterministic: same seed produces identical jittered output
Different seeds produce different jittered positions
Jitter displacement is bounded by 0.35 * cellSize per axis
voidDensity > 0 produces fewer edges than the full grid formula
voidDensity=0 preserves all edges
Voided lattice has fewer vertices than the full grid (vertex compaction)
Voided lattice output includes connectivity Float32Array with correct length
Connectivity values are positive integers (edge count per vertex)
Full grid (no void) also produces connectivity data
Voided lattice edge positions are all finite
Void determinism: same seed+voidDensity produces identical edge/vertex counts
Edge array lengths remain consistent after voiding (edgeCount * 2 * 3)
Vertex array lengths remain consistent after voiding (vertexCount * 3)
Backward compatibility: calling without seed/jitter/voidDensity still works
Points geometry has aConnectivity attribute with itemSize 1
Vertex dot vertex shader declares aConnectivity attribute
Vertex dot shader passes vConnectivity varying to fragment shader
Edge vertex shader does NOT contain treble micro displacement lines
Vertex dot vertex shader does NOT contain treble micro displacement
Edge shader still contains treble in fragment for lightness (not removed)
Vertex dot shader still contains treble sparkle in point size calculation
Slow modulation is gated behind bass smoothstep (contains smoothstep near uBassEnergy in slow modulation block)
Bass drift coefficient is reduced from 0.25 to 0.15
uBasePointSize default is increased to 0.06
CubeLatticeConfig accepts jitter and voidDensity options
Seed is threaded to generator (different seeds produce different geometry counts with voiding)
With voidDensity, edge count is less than the full grid formula
System still passes existing init/draw/cleanup lifecycle with new config
QualityProfile includes latticeVoidDensity field
QualityProfile includes latticeJitter field
latticeVoidDensity per tier: low=0.4, medium=0.3, high=0.25
latticeJitter per tier: low=0.25, medium=0.3, high=0.35
latticeVoidDensity decreases from low to high (lower tiers get more voids for perf)
latticeJitter increases from low to high tier
extractSystemConfig cubelattice includes jitter and voidDensity
